name: Workable API
what_it_does: Workable is a hiring and recruitment platform with a REST API for managing job postings, candidates, interview pipelines, and offer letters.
use_cases: Agents sourcing and syncing candidate data from Workable into internal systems or data warehouses
Automated job posting — agents creating and updating job listings across departments based on headcount plans
Interview scheduling automation — agents retrieving interview stages and updating candidate statuses
Offer letter workflows — agents triggering offer generation and tracking acceptance status
Recruiting analytics — agents pulling pipeline metrics, time-to-hire, and stage conversion rates
not_for: Payroll or onboarding beyond basic handoff — Workable stops at offer acceptance
Complex HRIS management — use Personio, HiBob, or BambooHR for full employee lifecycle
High-volume bulk candidate imports — rate limits (10 req/sec) constrain bulk operations
best_when: Best when your company uses Workable as its ATS and agents need to automate recruiting workflows like job posting, candidate status updates, or pipeline reporting.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need full HRIS functionality beyond hiring, or when bulk candidate operations exceed rate limit tolerance.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for partner integrations; API key (Bearer token) for direct access. Scopes control access to candidates, jobs, and account data. Data processing agreement required for accessing candidate PII.

free_tier_limits.note: 15-day free trial, no credit card required

pricing.notes: Trial allows API access. Production API requires paid plan. Pricing is per job slot or per user depending on plan.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API is well-documented with clear endpoint reference. Error messages are generally descriptive but can be generic for validation failures. Pagination is cursor-based and well-specified.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET operations are idempotent. Candidate creation is not idempotent — duplicate submissions create duplicate records. Use external candidate ID to check for existence before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit is 10 req/sec — agents doing bulk operations must implement throttling or risk 429 errors
Candidate data access requires a signed Data Processing Agreement (DPA) with Workable before production use
OAuth2 tokens expire — agents must implement token refresh; access tokens are short-lived
Job shortcode (not ID) is the primary key used in many endpoints — store shortcodes, not numeric IDs
Webhook payloads do not include full candidate data — agents must make follow-up GET requests to retrieve details

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant with EU data residency option. OAuth2 with scopes for partner access. DPA required for candidate PII — appropriate data governance for recruiting data.
